Glucose [Mass/volume] in Urine by Test strip

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows evidence of significant renal impairment (elevated creatinine, proteinuria, low urine specific gravity), mild hypoglycemia, and abnormal liver enzyme (alkaline phosphatase). Urinalysis reveals glucosuria, ketonuria, and proteinuria without signs of infection. These findings suggest possible diabetic nephropathy or another chronic kidney disease, with possible hepatic involvement. Clinical correlation and further evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
